skill.name: statistical-analysis
skill.description: Plan, run, diagnose, and report statistical analyses for academic and research data using tests, effect sizes, power analysis, and APA-style reporting.
skill.summary: Guides statistical test selection, assumption checks, effect sizes, power analysis, Bayesian methods, and APA-style reporting.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but AI review found them to be false positives in statistical documentation, Markdown code fences, normal Python import examples, and public reference URLs. No evidence found of prompt injection, malicious intent, credential handling, shell execution, data exfiltration, or unauthorized network behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to Markdown code fences and inline references in statistical guidance. Reviewed locations are documentation examples, not Ruby backtick execution or shell invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Very low confidence as a true security issue. The flagged tokens occur in Markdown fences, inline filenames, and Python examples rather than executable shell calls.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Statistical Text Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings are statistical terms, APA examples, and documentation text such as means, standard deviations, model diagnostics, and headings. No cryptographic API or weak algorithm use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Very low confidence as a true security issue. Context shows statistical notation and prose, not MD5, SHA-1, DES, or other cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Static Python Imports Flagged as Dynamic Imports
low_findings.description: The script findings are normal Python import statements inside fenced examples. They import statistical libraries and helper functions, not attacker-controlled modules or runtime dynamic imports.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Very low confidence as a dynamic import threat. The reviewed lines use static imports in documentation examples and do not evaluate user-controlled module names.

low_findings.title: Low Risk: Public Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: The network findings are public documentation or reproducibility example URLs. They do not transmit data, call remote APIs, or include credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Low confidence as a true network risk. The URLs are human-readable references in Markdown, with no executable request code or sensitive data flow.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Statistical Terms Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings are statistical or reporting text, including residuals, p-values, procedure descriptions, and headings. No system identity, process, host, or filesystem reconnaissance behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Very low confidence as a reconnaissance issue. The flagged lines are academic reporting content and statistical variable names, not commands that inspect the runtime environment.

content.user_title: Analyze Research Data with Statistical Tests
content.value_statement: Researchers need accurate test selection, assumption checks, and reporting. This skill guides statistical analysis, effect sizing, power planning, Bayesian methods, and APA-style summaries.
actual_capabilities: Selects appropriate statistical tests for group comparisons, relationships, and categorical outcomes.
Checks assumptions for normality, homogeneity of variance, linearity, and outliers.
Guides t-tests, ANOVA, chi-square tests, correlations, regressions, and non-parametric alternatives.
Calculates and interprets effect sizes with confidence intervals and practical significance.
Supports Bayesian analysis concepts, Bayes factors, priors, credible intervals, and convergence checks.
Drafts APA-style results sections, tables, figures, and reporting checklists.
limitations: It does not replace statistical review by a qualified methodologist.
It depends on correct data labels, study design details, and research questions.
It may require local Python packages for automated assumption checks and plots.
It cannot verify raw data quality without access to the dataset and study protocol.

use_cases.title: Plan a Research Analysis
use_cases.description: Choose tests, define assumptions, plan effect sizes, and estimate sample size before collecting data.

use_cases.title: Audit Study Results
use_cases.description: Review statistical outputs for missing assumptions, incomplete reporting, weak interpretation, and practical significance.

use_cases.title: Prepare Publication Reporting
use_cases.description: Convert analysis results into APA-style text with test statistics, confidence intervals, and transparent limitations.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Test
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner test selection for a simple study design.
prompt_templates.prompt: I have one outcome variable and two independent groups. Help me choose the right statistical test, list assumptions, and explain what information you need from my dataset.

prompt_templates.title: Check Assumptions
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my study design and variables. Create an assumption-checking plan for normality, variance equality, outliers, and independence before I run the main analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Interpret Results
prompt_templates.scenario: Turn statistical output into a clear research interpretation.
prompt_templates.prompt: I ran an ANOVA with post-hoc tests. Help me interpret the test statistic, p-values, effect sizes, confidence intervals, and practical significance.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Frequentist and Bayesian Results
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare my frequentist and Bayesian results for the same hypothesis. Explain agreement, disagreement, uncertainty, Bayes factors, and reporting implications.

output_examples.input: A researcher has two independent groups, one continuous score, and possible unequal variances.
output_examples.output: Recommended analysis: independent samples t-test with Welch correction if variances differ. Check normality by group, inspect outliers, report means, standard deviations, confidence intervals, p-value, and Cohen d.

output_examples.input: A reviewer asks whether a manuscript reports enough detail for a regression model.
output: Include sample size, predictors, outcome, model fit, coefficients, standard errors, confidence intervals, p-values, and diagnostics.
Report assumptions for linearity, residual behavior, influential cases, and multicollinearity.
Separate statistical significance from practical importance.

output_examples.input: A team wants to plan a study before collecting data.
output_examples.output: Start with the primary hypothesis, expected effect size, alpha level, desired power, and allocation ratio. Then run a priori power analysis and document the stopping rule.

best_practices: Define hypotheses, variables, and primary outcomes before selecting tests.
Check assumptions and diagnostics before interpreting p-values or model coefficients.
Report effect sizes, intervals, non-significant results, and analysis decisions transparently.
anti_patterns: Do not test many approaches until one result becomes significant.
Do not choose tests without matching the study design and measurement scale.
Do not report p-values without effect sizes, intervals, assumptions, and sample sizes.

faq.question: Can this skill choose the right statistical test?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses study design, outcome type, group structure, distribution, and research question to recommend appropriate tests.

faq.question: Does it run statistical calculations automatically?
faq.answer: It includes Python guidance and an assumption-checking script. Calculations require suitable local data and required Python packages.

faq.question: Can it help with APA reporting?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides reporting elements, result templates, table guidance, and language for common statistical tests.

faq.question: Does it support Bayesian statistics?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers priors, Bayes factors, credible intervals, posterior checks, and Bayesian alternatives to common tests.

faq.question: Is it suitable for clinical or regulated decisions?
faq.answer: Use it as analysis support only. Clinical, legal, or regulated studies need review by qualified domain and statistical experts.

faq.question: What data does it need?
faq.answer: It needs the research question, study design, variables, measurement scales, sample sizes, missing data details, and relevant output.
